title: moraea lazulina
intro: Moraea lazulina, also known as the blue gladiolus or blue corn lily, is a flowering plant native to the western and eastern parts of South Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Moraea lazulina include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as a treatment for snake bites and insect stings. Herbal preparations of Moraea lazulina can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using its roots to make a medicinal tea.
